summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened with a prayer for peace in Israel, encouraging the church to pray against terrorism and evil, citing Psalms 122:6-9. He then transitioned to the main message from Mark 10:46-52, the story of the blind beggar Bartimaeus. Pastor Scott used this narrative to illustrate that to see God's blessings and breakthrough, we must be willing to 'drop' the things holding us back. He taught that we must drop our 'cane'—our concern for what others think—and stop projecting a false image. Next, we must drop our 'coat'—the comfort zones and survival mechanisms we cling to that keep us from running to Jesus. Finally, we must drop our 'cup'—the hidden hurts, doubts, and things we hold onto that prevent God from filling our hands with His blessings. The message emphasized that faith precedes miracle, and true change comes when we surrender our burdens to Christ. The service concluded with a powerful call for salvation, where many made decisions to follow Jesus.
mainPoints: To see God's blessing, we must drop our 'cane'—our preoccupation with what other people think and the false images we project.
To receive breakthrough, we must drop our 'coat'—the comfort zones and survival mechanisms that keep us from fully running to Jesus.
To experience healing, we must drop our 'cup'—the hidden hurts, doubts, and things we cling to that prevent God from filling our hands with His provision.

versesMentioned.text: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: 'May those who love you be secure. May there be peace within your walls and security within your citadels.' For the sake of my family and friends, I will say, 'Peace be within you.' For the sake of the house of the LORD our God, I will seek your prosperity.

Then they came to Jericho. As Jesus and his disciples, together with a large crowd, were leaving the city, a blind man, Bartimaeus (which means 'son of Timaeus'), was sitting by the roadside begging. When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout, 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!' Many rebuked him and told him to be quiet, but he shouted all the more, 'Son of David, have mercy on me!' Jesus stopped and said, 'Call him.' So they called to the blind man, 'Cheer up! On your feet! He's calling you.' Throwing his cloak aside, he jumped to his feet and came to Jesus. 'What do you want me to do for you?' Jesus asked him. The blind man said, 'Rabbi, I want to see.' 'Go,' said Jesus, 'your faith has healed you.' Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus along the road.

Monday.scripture: Mark 10:47 - 'When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"'
Monday.focus: Identify your 'cane'—what are you most worried others think about you?
Monday.action: Write down one area where you project a false image to please people. Commit to being authentic in that area today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to care more about Your approval than anyone else's. Give me the courage to drop my 'cane' and be real.

Tuesday.scripture: Mark 10:50 - 'Throwing his cloak aside, he jumped to his feet and came to Jesus.'
Tuesday.focus: Identify your 'coat'—a comfort zone or survival habit you rely on.
Tuesday.action: Take one small, deliberate step outside of that comfort zone today. It could be a difficult conversation or a new act of faith.
Tuesday.prayer: God, my comfort zone is holding me back. I choose to drop my 'coat' and run to You. Give me strength to step out in faith.

Wednesday.scripture: Mark 10:51 - 'What do you want me to do for you?' Jesus asked him. The blind man said, 'Rabbi, I want to see.'
Wednesday.focus: Identify your 'cup'—a hidden hurt, doubt, or sin you're holding onto.
Wednesday.action: In a private moment, name that thing aloud to God. Symbolically let it go by writing it down and tearing up the paper.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I've been holding onto ___. I drop my 'cup' today. I believe Your promise that my faith will heal me. Fill my empty hands with Your blessing.

Thursday.scripture: Mark 10:52 - 'Go,' said Jesus, 'your faith has healed you.' Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus along the road.
Thursday.focus: The power of faith that leads to action.
Thursday.action: Act on a promise God has given you, even in a small way. Do something today that demonstrates your faith in His word.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I believe You are a miracle-working God. My faith is in You. Help me to act on that faith and follow You closely today.

Friday.scripture: Psalms 122:6 - 'Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: "May those who love you be secure."'
Friday.focus: Praying with a sound voice for global peace.
Friday.action: Set aside time to pray specifically for peace in Israel and for innocent people affected by conflict.
Friday.prayer: God of peace, I pray for Your protection and comfort over Israel. Let Your justice prevail over evil and terrorism. Guide my words and heart.

Saturday.scripture: Mark 10:52 - 'Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus along the road.'
Saturday.focus: Living in the new sight and freedom Christ provides.
Saturday.action: Reflect on one area where you've experienced new 'sight' or freedom this week. Share a word of testimony or encouragement with someone.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for healing me and giving me new vision. As I follow You, use my story to encourage others to drop their burdens and run to You.
